text.title: Unit Testing (FIRST + AAA) Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when unit-testing is applied incorrectly. Each entry has a symptom (`description`), root cause (`reason`), and corrective fix (in `note`).

antipattern.reason: Tests hit real DB or do real I/O; not isolated.
antipattern.note: Fix: Move to in-memory doubles or move the tests to the integration layer.

antipattern.description: Refactor passes review, but 30 tests break with no behavior change.
antipattern.reason: Tests assert on private state via reflection.
antipattern.note: Fix: Rewrite assertions to observe public API only; if behavior is unobservable, the API is missing a hook.

antipattern.reason: Test depends on system clock or random seed.
antipattern.note: Fix: Inject a clock; seed RNG; use freezegun / fakerandom.

antipattern.reason: One test is asserting an entire workflow.
antipattern.note: Fix: Split into 4 focused tests; use a Builder for shared input.
